title: User adds same item on two lines — SP raises duplicate error, bot offers fix
user_persona: Buyer who tries to split an item across two lines for different need dates

conversation.user: Create a PO for SUP-100, USD, today, buyer BUY-5. Lines: ITM-200 50 units at 10 needed next week, and ITM-200 30 units at 10 needed next month.

bot_must: warn that SP hdrchk has duplicate-item check (matches the IF EXISTS po_poitm_item_detail branch) — same item code on two lines will raise error 1025311
offer alternatives: combine into one line, or use a schedule (POCRTMAINLNK2) to split delivery by date
explain Schedule sub-screen (POCRTMAINLNK2 → PoCrtSch): keeps one line but splits the quantity across multiple delivery dates
say this is the right pattern for staggered delivery of the same item

conversation.user: Use schedule. One line ITM-200, 80 total. Schedule 50 next week, 30 next month.

bot_must: return to main and ask to commit
fire POCRTMAINSBT — no duplicate error since only one line

evaluation_metrics.duplicate_detected_pre_commit: bot warns before commit, not after SP error
evaluation_metrics.alternative_offered: Schedule splice presented as the right pattern
evaluation_metrics.splice_walked: Schedule sub-screen used to split delivery
